data.title: The Bhutanese government transferred 319.7 BTC to two wallets; some of the addresses had previously been used for sale on exchanges.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, the Royal Government of Bhutan has transferred 319.7 BTC, worth approximately $22.67 million, to two wallets. One of the wallets is a new address, suspected to be linked to an exchange; the other address was previously used for fund transfers and sales through OKX or Galaxy Digital.

data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index Retreats After Previous Surge
data.description:
On April 9, Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index experienced a pullback following a significant rise of nearly 800 points the previous day. According to Jin10, the index opened over 100 points lower in the morning and briefly fell more than 200 points to reach 25,653.63 points before recovering slightly. By midday, the Hang Seng Index closed down 0.35% at 25,803.12 points, while the Hang Seng Tech Index fell 1.26% to 4,861.39 points. The total market turnover for the Hang Seng Index reached HKD 126.652 billion.

In terms of sector performance, optical communication and computing infrastructure stocks led gains, while the innovative drug concept saw a brief surge in early trading. However, most technology stocks underwent a correction, with real estate and gold stocks experiencing notable declines. Among individual stocks, Yangtze Optical Fibre and Cable Joint Stock Limited Company (06869.HK) rose 3.2%, WuXi AppTec Co., Ltd. (02359.HK) increased by 3.53%, Kingdee International Software Group Company Limited (00268.HK) fell 5.49%, Kuaishou Technology (01024.HK) dropped 3.55%, and Longfor Group Holdings Limited (00960.HK) decreased by 4.07%.

data.title: U.S. Vice President Advocates Peaceful Nationalism in Hungary Amid Global Tensions
data.description: U.S. Vice President Donald Trump has expressed support for Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban by promoting a vision of peaceful nationalism during his visit to Hungary. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the vice president's message, which contrasts sharply with the ongoing conflict in Iran. The advocacy for nationalism comes at a time when global tensions are heightened, raising questions about the feasibility of such an approach in the current geopolitical climate. Critics argue that the situation in Iran undermines the viability of peaceful nationalism, as international conflicts continue to pose significant challenges. The vice president's remarks aim to strengthen diplomatic ties with Hungary, emphasizing shared values and mutual interests. However, the backdrop of the Iran war presents a complex scenario for implementing nationalist policies focused on peace.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $18.6319 million yesterday, with Fidelity's FETH ETF experiencing the largest net outflow at $32.4311 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $18.6319 million yesterday (April 8th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the BlackRock Stalled ETH ETF (ETHB), with a net inflow of $44.2303 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $356 million. This was followed by the 21Shares ETF (TETH), with a net inflow of $1.9809 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $25.5027 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the Fidelity ETF (FETH), with a net outflow of $32.4311 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $2.251 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of the Ethereum spot ETF is $12.558 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.71%, and a cumulative net inflow of $11.518 billion.

data.title: Malaysia's February Industrial Output Growth Falls Short of Expectations
data.description: Malaysia's industrial production in February recorded a year-on-year increase of 3.1%, according to Jin10. This growth rate was below analysts' expectations, which had projected a 5.2% rise. The lower-than-expected performance highlights potential challenges in the country's industrial sector.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Urges Congress to Pass CLARITY Act for Digital Asset Regulation
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Besant has called on Congress to swiftly pass the CLARITY Act to establish a clear regulatory framework for digital assets. According to Foresight News, the global market value of digital assets has fluctuated between $2 trillion and $3 trillion over the past year, with approximately one-sixth of Americans holding some form of digital asset. The application of blockchain technology in payments, settlements, and physical asset exchanges continues to expand. Cryptocurrencies are no longer niche experiments but are widely adopted technologies globally. The United States must take proactive measures to continue leading in the establishment of rules in this field.

data.title: EU to Lower Economic Growth Forecast, Says Dombrovskis
data.description: The European Commission is set to revise its economic growth forecast for this year downward, according to Valdis Dombrovskis, Executive Vice President of the European Commission. According to Jin10, Dombrovskis highlighted the need for adjustments in light of current economic conditions. The decision reflects ongoing challenges within the European economy, necessitating a reassessment of growth expectations. This move comes amid broader economic uncertainties affecting the region.

data.title: Japan's Five-Year Bond Auction Sees Stable Demand Amid Iran Ceasefire
data.description: Japan's recent five-year government bond auction experienced demand consistent with the 12-month average. Bloomberg posted on X that the temporary ceasefire in the Iran conflict contributed to stabilizing investor interest. The auction results indicate a steady appetite for Japanese government securities, reflecting broader market sentiments influenced by geopolitical developments.

data.title: Arthur Hayes Skeptical of Iran's Alleged Bitcoin Toll Collection
data.description: Arthur Hayes expressed skepticism about Iran's purported acceptance of Bitcoin for toll payments on April 9. According to BlockBeats, Hayes stated that he would only believe Iran is collecting tolls in Bitcoin if he sees actual transactions on the Bitcoin blockchain. Otherwise, he considers it a tactic by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps to mock the Western fiat currency financial system.

data.title: Ju.com's "Second Round of Meme IPO Plan" Phase 3 project ZX820RR has officially launched, and Phase 4 FLUFFY will open for subscription today at 16:00.
data.description: The third phase of ZX820RR has officially launched, with a subscription rate of 2.8%, a listing price of 0.132 USDT, a new offering yield of 32%, and an overall return on investment of approximately 1%. There is no lock-up period after settlement of ZX820RR subscriptions; the tokens are immediately available for spot purchase, and users can choose to sell or withdraw immediately. The fourth phase of FLUFFY will officially open for subscription today at 16:00 and will end on April 11th at 16:00. This phase continues the multi-currency participation mechanism, allowing users to participate using JU/USDT/BTC/ETH, with the platform uniformly converting and allocating tokens in USDT. This round also introduces a dynamic subscription rate model; for every additional 5,000,000 USDT raised, the subscription rate increases by 0.2%. Successfully inviting friends to participate will allow you to share up to 20% of the final issuance in token rewards.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $125 million yesterday, with Fidelity FBTC experiencing the largest net outflow at $79.12 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $125 million yesterday (April 8th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was BlackRock ETF IBIT, with a net inflow of $40.376 million. IBIT's historical total net inflow has reached $63.32 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was Fidelity ETF FBTC, with a net outflow of $79.1201 million. FBTC's historical total net inflow has reached $10.981 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $91.901 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.43%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $56.145 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin Wallet Rescue Tool Prototype Unveiled Amid Quantum Attack Concerns
data.description: Lightning Labs CTO Olaoluwa Osuntokun has introduced a prototype for a Bitcoin wallet rescue tool. According to NS3.AI, the tool aims to assist Taproot wallets in recovering funds if Bitcoin's current signature system is disabled due to quantum attack threats. Proof generation for the prototype took approximately 55 seconds on a high-end consumer MacBook. Despite the development, there is no formal proposal or deployment timeline, and developers are divided on the urgency of the quantum threat.

data.title: Arthur Hayes: Claims that Iran charges tolls in Bitcoin lack evidence.
data.description: Arthur Hayes stated that he would only believe the claim that Iran was charging transit fees in BTC if he saw actual Bitcoin transaction records on the blockchain; otherwise, it was more likely a propaganda campaign targeting the Western financial system.

data.title: Bithumb has not recovered some of the mistakenly sent Bitcoins and has initiated asset preservation procedures.
data.description: South Korean exchange Bithumb has initiated asset seizure proceedings against affected users after failing to recover a portion of Bitcoin mistakenly distributed. The incident occurred during a February rewards distribution event. The platform planned to distribute a total of 620,000 won in rewards to 249 users, but an operational error resulted in the distribution being in Bitcoin. Although the platform reversed the operation within minutes, some users had already completed transactions or transferred their assets, resulting in approximately 12.3 billion won worth of assets remaining unrecovered. The platform subsequently contacted each user, and most funds have been returned; however, some users have refused to return their funds.

data.title: Giggles Secures $1.2 Million in Pre-Seed Funding
data.description:
Giggles has successfully raised $1.2 million in a pre-seed funding round. According to Odaily, the investment was led by 1kx Capital, Virtuals Protocol, and Social Graph Ventures, with participation from angel investors associated with OpenAI and Bain Capital.

Giggles is described as a social application similar to TikTok, allowing users to 'trade' content by integrating information dissemination with market mechanisms. The project launched its beta version in August 2025, attracting approximately 150,000 users who have engaged in content trading across about 10,000 videos. Currently, there is a waiting list of 400,000 people, and the platform plans to officially launch in the United States soon.

data.title: North Korean IT Workers Allegedly Earn Millions Using Fake Identities
data.description: Documents from a compromised device reveal that a team of 140 North Korean IT workers has reportedly earned approximately $1 million monthly and $3.5 million in cryptocurrency since late November by using fake identities to secure developer jobs. According to NS3.AI, ZachXBT, a blockchain investigator, noted that some users on the group's payment site appeared to be connected to entities sanctioned by the U.S. Office of Foreign Assets Control. Additionally, ZachXBT traced the payment flow to Chinese bank accounts and North Korean wallets, which Tether blacklisted in December.

data.title: Social platform Giggles raises $1.2 million in pre-seed funding.
data.description: Giggles has raised $1.2 million in pre-seed funding from investors including 1kx Capital, Virtuals Protocol, Social Graph Ventures, and angel investors with backgrounds in OpenAI and Bain Capital. Positioned as a social application similar to TikTok, Giggles allows users to "trade" content, combining information dissemination with market mechanisms. The project launched its beta version in August 2025 and has already attracted approximately 150,000 users to participate in content trading, covering about 10,000 videos. Currently, there are 400,000 people on the waiting list, and the official launch in the US is planned for soon.

data.title: Bitcoin Leads Social Dominance, Ethereum Tops AI Search Volume
data.description: Bitcoin has emerged as the leader in social dominance with a share of 34.35%, followed by Ethereum at 11.65%. According to NS3.AI, Ethereum also leads the AI search volume list with an 11% share, while the Solana Foundation ranks second at 10%.

data.title: AI Platform Surf Introduces Tool for Cryptocurrency Data Analysis
data.description:
On April 9, Surf, a specialized AI platform for the cryptocurrency sector, announced the launch of its Surf Skill CLI tool. According to BlockBeats, this tool enables AI agents like Claude Code to directly access cryptocurrency data, potentially replacing over 60 independent APIs.

Once users install the tool, AI agents can provide real-time market analysis, on-chain DeFi TVL, and social trend insights, offering structured insights into the crypto market.

Additionally, SurfAI, as a crypto-native AI research platform, has made available a $200,000 API credit limit for free. This initiative is compatible with various agent environments, aiming to streamline the workflow between developers and AI in the cryptocurrency domain.

data.title: Trump Denounces Reports on Iran Negotiation Plan as False
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has criticized reports from The New York Times and CNN regarding a ten-point plan on Iran negotiations. According to Odaily, Trump labeled these reports as entirely fabricated and aimed at discrediting those involved in the peace process. He dismissed the points as fraudulent schemes and called the media outlets 'evil losers.' Trump concluded his statement with a call to 'Make America Great Again.'
